channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: FA Cup stunner: Liverpool is knocked out by second-tier Plymouth in fourth round
item.description: Premier League leader Liverpool was knocked out of the FA Cup by second-tier struggler Plymouth after losing 1-0 in a stunning fourth-round upset on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tom Brady reveals he took 15-minute nap before playing in 1st Super Bowl
item.description: Tom Brady revealed in a clip from “The Madden Cruiser Tour: A Bayou Adventure with Bill Belichick" that he took a nap before his first Super Bowl appearance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles' path to the Super Bowl was paved by the prowess of their offensive line
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Travis Kelce is an unusual example of an offensive star who has a keen interest in his next opponent’s offensive line.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bucks announce Giannis Antetokounmpo out of All-Star Game with injury
item.description: Milwaukee forward Giannis Antetokounmpo will not play in next weekend's All-Star Game because of a strained left calf, meaning NBA Commissioner Adam Silver will have to pick a replacement.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Big Game Bound: Game day special breaks down QB matchup, Chiefs-Eagles live from New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS -- Super Bowl Sunday has arrived--and we've reached the final destination for this season's "Big Game Bound!" Chris Hagan is live from outside the Caesars Superdome, site of Super Bowl LIX between the Kansas City Chiefs and the Philadelphia Eagles. Kickoff is set for 6:30 p.m. ET on FOX.
item.dc:creator: Phil Nardiello - Senior Sports Producer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heat officially welcome Wiggins, Mitchell and Anderson to the team after last week's Butler trade
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Things rarely happen in the NBA at 8:57 a.m. on a Sunday. But that was the moment when Miami's newest acquisitions — Andrew Wiggins, Davion Mitchell and Kyle Anderson — walked into the Heat interview room for the first time. The reason was simple: There's a lot of work to do.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce spotted out in New Orleans
item.description: New Orleans resident Geoff Jacques said he saw Swift and Kelce as well as Patrick Mahomes and his wife who were with Swift and Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, NewsNation

item.title: Clippers dominate fourth quarter in win over Jazz
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - The Los Angeles Clippers broke open a close game in the fourth quarter, outscoring the Jazz 36-24, en route to a 130-110 win over Utah Saturday night. Jordan Clarkson led the Jazz with 24 points, while Keyonte George added 21 off the bench.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Curry scores 34 points, Butler adds 25 in Golden State debut as Warriors rally to beat Bulls 132-111
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Stephen Curry scored 34 points and tied a season high with eight 3-pointers, Jimmy Butler added 25 points in his Golden State debut and the Warriors rallied to beat the Chicago Bulls 132-111 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dolphins' Tyreek Hill doubles down on 'love' for Miami, despite 'frustration' with regular season results
item.description: The Miami Dolphins experienced some turbulence during this past season, and ultimately fell short of the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utes offense struggles again in loss to West Virginia
item.description: MORGANTOWN, W.V. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah basketball team shot just 41 percent from the field and lost its third straight road game at West Virginia Saturday, 72-61.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Thomas Detry shoots 65 to take 5-shot lead into the final round of the Phoenix Open
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Thomas Detry shot a 6-under 65 on Saturday to take a five-shot lead into the final round of the Phoenix Open as he chases his first PGA Tour victory.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Padres sign outfielder/first baseman Connor Joe for 2025 season
item.description: The San Diego Padres have made a move to bolster their roster for the upcoming season by signing outfielder and first baseman Connor Joe to a one-year contract, President of Baseball Operations and General Manager A.J. Preller announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how the new NBA All-Star Game format works
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The NBA All-Star Game is coming to Chase Center for the first time on Feb. 16, but the game will not look like the All-Star Games that NBA fans are accustomed to.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: McDavid makes perfect pass in overtime as the Oilers beat the Blackhawks 4-3
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor McDavid set up Zach Hyman in overtime, and the Edmonton Oilers beat the Chicago Blackhawks 4-3 on Wednesday night. Hyman redirected McDavid's pass for the power-play goal at 1:36. It was Hyman's 19th goal and McDavid's 49th assist of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: Tri City Christian 49, Army Navy 37
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great game on Monday night in Vista with Tri City Christian hosting Army Navy. All tied up at 21 at halftime, the Eagles pulled off and won 49-37. Lead by Caleb Williams who hit for 14. Army Navy was lead by Yanler Ramos, he also poured in 14.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: 70 Illinois residents can win Las Vegas vacation with Rosati's Pizza
item.description: Illinois residents can enter the Rosati's Pizza sweepstakes and win a three-day, two-night vacation at the Circa Resort and Casino in Las Vegas, with the contest closing on the day of the Big Game.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Travis Kelce fined for unsportsmanlike conduct in AFC title game
item.description: While there was no flag thrown on the play, Kelce got hit with an $11,255 fine for unsportsmanlike conduct after a one-yard Mahomes rushing touchdown
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, NewsNation

item.title: Tiger Woods' mother, Kultida Woods, has died
item.description: Kultida Woods, the Thai-born mother of Tiger Woods, died Tuesday morning, a week after being seen attending his indoor TMRW Golf League in Florida.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON AP Golf Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz lose heartbreaker to Indiana, 112-111
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz could not hold a 12-point lead in the final six minutes, and lose to the Indiana Pacers Monday night at the Delta Center, 112-111.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump welcomes Florida Panthers to the White House
item.description: The Florida Panthers were the first professional championship team to be celebrated at the White House under the new Trump administration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WFLA, NBC
